topic: Platform Upgrade — Caching, Performance, Mature Features
questionsAsked: What's the primary motivation — performance or new features?
Which mature features are most interesting?
constraints: Performance first — caching and faster loads are top priority
Wants all four feature areas: reports, forecasting, notifications, search
Existing stack: Next.js 16, React 19, Supabase, Tailwind 4

candidates.name: Cache-First Architecture
candidates.summary: Layer caching at three levels (React.cache, Next.js cacheTag, Supabase Realtime invalidation). Shared auth, Recharts. Stays on Vercel+Supabase.
risks: unstable_cache API churn in Next.js — mitigate with wrapper
No background jobs for notifications — needs separate solution
mvp: cacheTag on read actions, revalidateTag on mutations

candidates.name: Self-Hosted Platform (Redis + Workers)
candidates.summary: Docker deployment with Redis caching + BullMQ job queues. Background notifications, scheduled reports, optional Meilisearch for search.
risks: Ops burden — Docker, Redis, worker health monitoring
Self-hosting reliability — mitigate with restart policies
mvp: Docker compose with Next.js + Redis + worker
Redis cache adapter for cross-instance caching
Bill reminder emails via BullMQ + Resend

candidates.name: Progressive Enhancement (Pragmatic Middle)
candidates.summary: Use Supabase built-ins (pg_trgm, pg_cron, Edge Functions, Realtime) instead of self-hosting Redis/workers. Cache + Recharts + search + notifications with zero new infra.
risks: Supabase free-tier Edge Function limits — mitigate with batching
pg_cron coarser than BullMQ — mitigate with nightly batch approach
mvp: Nightly bill/budget alert emails via pg_cron + Edge Function

recommendation.rationale: Gets caching, Recharts, search, and notifications without adding Docker/Redis ops burden. Supabase already has pg_trgm, pg_cron, Edge Functions, and Realtime. Clean upgrade path to Option B if needed later.
